item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As the 420th upvote I declare this as the best scp

+1 obviously

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Reverted a recent edit as it was stylistic. Please ask staff or the original author for permission in the future before doing something like this again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels less like a justifiable SCP and more an item that should have been featured in that Sci-Fi miniseries, 'The Lost Room'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotta love a tothbrush that flips out if you don't brush regularly. However, if it were only to remove dead or inorganic matter, than surely it would be terrible at cleaning teeth seeing as plaque is technically alive.

item.title: Re: Is SCP-063 radioactive?
item.content:encoded:
so is SCP-063 radioactive??

Per the containment procedures:

Object is to be used as designed at least once in a twenty-four (24) hour period or the object will begin to emit an unknown specialized radiation that results in objects and material within a 0.6 m (2 ft) radius being slowly warped and eventually disintegrating into a fine dust. Radiation's effect on living test subjects has not been monitored.

Granted, that's more description than procedures, but this page was originally written over 11 years ago, before the site had the current quality standards.

item.title: Is SCP-063 radioactive?
item.content:encoded: No joke someone tagged this page as “radioactive” so is SCP-063 radioactive??

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel bad for all the people with braces because this would be so effective but it would also destroy the braces too.
maybe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: watch me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can you magnetize a toothbrush?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gotta agree and say that this has aged like milk left out in the sun.

Stuff like "number (#)", "appears to be" and that this is kept in a Doctors bathroom really bug me. Would like to see this rewritten, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jesus, this did not age well. It could use a good rewriting. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with a lot of people, this SCP needs rewriting badly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This seems to be more of an Anomolous Object, but still. It's funny and unique.

Upvoted +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, I feel like this hasn't aged particularly well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do you really think that giving Class D personnel a thing that can make holes in the walls is a good idea?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Feel like the whole radiation thing could be gotten rid of and just left unexplained also if it really does have to be used then place it in the D-class dormitories and sa that whoever uses doesn't have to be sent to Keter duty that day

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is any connection with the Egyptian god Thoth intended?

Also, out of all possibilities, I'm most curious about what happens if you feed it to 914.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
headcanon: 063 is a motherfucking jojo reference.

Also what Cream are we talking about here? Is it another thing from jojo or are we talking the new David Firth short?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me of The Hand from JoJo's Bizarre Adventure, while 2207 reminds me of Cream,

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired: Edit has been made. This can be reverted by the author at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 72 hours if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
- hide block

Since SCP-063 is extremely short and its anomalous property is relatively straight forward, I think that a link to SCP-2207 would be appropriate. SCP-2207 has a lot going for it narrative wise, and both objects are made of plastic and are able to directly affect/remove whatever they touch, so I feel as if this would be enough to tie them together.

Editing this passage:

SCP-063 displays the ability to effortlessly cleave through any and all dead or inorganic matter, the focal point of this ability being the bristles. However, matter touched by the bristles is not separated, such as by way of a knife, but completely expunged from existence, leaving no trace whatsoever. Additionally, subjects who have used SCP-063 have claimed that the experience left their teeth feeling remarkably clean. In spite of its extraordinary abilities, lab analysis has discerned that SCP-063 is completely made of common plastic.

To read:

SCP-063 displays the ability to effortlessly cleave through any and all dead or inorganic matter, the focal point of this ability being the bristles. However, matter touched by the bristles is not separated, such as by way of a knife, but completely expunged from existence, leaving no trace whatsoever. This mode of operation is reminiscent of SCP-2207, suggesting the two anomalies share a connection or were created by the same entity or entities. Additionally, subjects who have used SCP-063 have claimed that the experience left their teeth feeling remarkably clean. In spite of its extraordinary abilities, lab analysis has discerned that SCP-063 is completely made of common plastic.

Additionally, adding this in there provides a little more in the means of backstory for SCP-063, and also allows for the reader to speculate on what happens with the material expunged from reality by SCP-063.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually somehow skipped over the whole daily radiation thing on my first read, and I agree that whole aspect seems entirely unnecessary. I was a lot more fond of this thing when I thought was just a simple tothbrush.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know this is an oldie and a goodie, but I feel like it would be overall improved without the spooky radiation and the containment procedures were just "keep it in a locker".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Obligatory "use it on 682"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Staff notice: This user has been permanently banned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Only if you're not careful.

Might irreperably damage your first few minis until you got the hang if it, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wouldn't it erase the mini?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want this thing. Stripping paint off of miniatures would be a breeze!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I like the article, it does have its flaws. I'm guessing it's simply because of its age. We've come a long way since then.

What also irked me was the final line about the thief killing himself after being questioned. I don't know, it just feels out of place, and a bit of a cheap way to hide its origins.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gah. "Contained" in a personal office, the radiation feels tacked on to justify "containing" it in a personal office, so much science fail it's not even funny…why is this one so highly rated again?

If it needs to be used, it should be used in a containment cell of some sort. After all, what if Doctor <Blank> has to take a sick day?

Downvoted, hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"the object will begin to emit an unknown specialized radiation that results in objects and material within a 0.6 m (2 ft) radius being slowly warped and eventually disintegrating into a fine dust. Radiation's effect on living test subjects has not been monitored."

Shouldn't this probably be in the Description?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Object is to be used as designed at least once in a twenty-four (24) hour period or the object will begin to emit an unknown specialized radiation that results in objects and material within a 0.6 m (2 ft) radius being slowly warped and eventually disintegrating into a fine dust

So…if you left it alone it would disintegrate everything…then, unless it is not affected by gravity…it would fall down and continue? Far down into Earth? That's some serious Tothbrushing going on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, I'm surprised this has so many upvotes. It's in severe need of editing.
1) Enamel is inorganic
2) It'd be better if the Kingdom Eubacteria was included, not just inorganic
3) The "radiation" bit doesn't belong in the procedures
4) "Cleave" doesn't work there
Nice title, and generally a decent idea, but please fix it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes! Yes they should.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know I'm late, but wouldn't using magnets or something to keep it levitated be a completely reliable way of keeping it from destroying everything?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i know this is old and im probably beating a dead horse but what kind of safes are made out of organic material? id recommend changing it to something like someone working in a slaughter house using it to clean bones

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is probably my favorite short one. A toothbrush that destroys things if you don't use it! Brilliant!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't the emissions be mentioned elsewhere but the containment procedures?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just don't "get" this one. Maybe I'm missing something, but it seems to me to just be a toothbrush that works really well. There are some interesting tidbits, like its misspelled name and emitting radiation if not used, but those aren't enough to really make it interesting, in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this cause, imo, it expresses the importance of brushing your teeth daily, and the radiation emitted from inactivity is a symbolism of plague and cavities building from not brushing. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No need for toothpaste? What about bacteria buildup! How unsanitary!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is… rather bad.

The term "cleave" feels weird, and the later explanation confirms that it is flat out wrong.

The object special property is extremely "overpowered" and, ironically (and apparently not intentionally), really bad at what it is advertised as. Most of the thing we want to clean from our teeth are organic, after all.

Most importantly, I don't find anything interesting or intriguing about this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What the…
The name was the only thing I liked in this article.
Wouldn't mind at all if this was

completely expunged from existence, leaving no trace whatsoever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Found this, should be nice at an example of visible radiation when the toothbrush surpases the 24h of inactivity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can it be used to clean up the goo created by 035?

Cleaning up after 035 with the tothbrush - that would be a sucky job. XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, "Safe" doesn't mean it's harmless; it just means that it's predictable and easily contained. This does technically fail the Locked Box test, which suggests that it could be Euclid, but Safe is frankly okay with me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, but it does not seem safe to me.

unknown specialized radiation that results in objects and material within a 0.6 m (2 ft) radius being slowly warped and eventually disintegrating into a fine dust. Radiation's effect on living test subjects has not been monitored.

I would make D class personnel use it, run more tests, and keep it locked away.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I might write a tale where the Church of the Broken God finds out about this…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, how about if it destroys all solid and liquid matter that is not human ? That'll make more sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If it only affects dead or inorganic matter, wouldn't it just pass over bacteria and plaque? Now, if it were "The World's Best Tothpaste" that'd be different. .

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see no reason not to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is incredibly nit-picky, but would it detract from the article if we were to put a [sic] in the quoted name of the brush?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"he took his own life"
[sarcasm]With the device?[/sarcasm]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That 24-hour period should clearly be reduced to 12, because everyone knows you need to brush twice daily.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The background info on this totally makes it. There's something about it that I can't quite put my finger on.

item.title: Re: Eh?
item.content:encoded: True. Also, I would like to point out that the subject would not "remain ignorant" but would "continue to profess ignorance".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This would be hell on fillings.

item.title: Eh?
item.content:encoded: "Cleave" isn't the right word for the process the tothbrush does, is it? It says later that matter is destroyed, not separated.
